body_txt: The practice of pressing a certain point on a person's body to achieve a certain effect (can also be multiple points in quick succession, or multiple points simultaneously). The most common effect is to paralyze the target or knock them unconscious. For knocking someone unconscious by the less subtle method of a strong blow to the head see Tap on the Head . For the more lethal version see Touch of Death . In martial arts, can overlap with Ki Manipulation , as ki/chi/qi flows in the body are supposedly the underlying mechanism of both pressure points and acupuncture according to certain Eastern practices. As a result, pressure points are oftentimes used as a sort of Mana Burn and/or Power Nullifier technique for ki-based attacks, where a blocked meridian on the target temporarily disables the use of certain powers or techniques. Pressure points are also handy if you are trying to avoid someone's death. When trying to stop severe bleeding from one of the extremities, applying pressure to the right area (typically farther up the limb near a joint) can significantly slow the flow of blood to an extremity, allowing time to dress the wound and seek medical care. In Wuxia stories and related media, expect these benefits to be exaggerated to superhuman levels, where a Martial Medic skilled in traditional medicine can use pressure points to cure illness, rapidly heal a patient's injuries , or augment someone's strength or agility by redirecting their ki . Although rarely portrayed in a realistic fashion, the existence of pressure points is decidedly Truth in Television . There are many points on the body that are particularly vulnerable such as nerve clusters, joints, blood vessels, the windpipe, the eyes and the groin . Striking with enough force or applying sufficient pressure to these areas can cause anything from pain and discomfort to severe structural damage , though said pressure typically exceeds greatly the classic finger-pinching shown in media and is much less reliable than it might sound. Many of these points work by pain compliance alone, which can turn almost useless in an all-out fight given that adrenaline makes humans able to ignore even traumatic mutilations. Often, pain compliance through the use of pressure points only works when it is accompanied by sufficient restraint, as attempting to use it on someone who has the strength to overpower those grabbing hold of them is largely ineffective, and typically results in a violently-enraged target who’s now free to unleash a beatdown of their own. This is before we get into the rather uncomfortable association pain compliance has with torture - the person administering the pain has somewhat of a free pass through plausible deniability to be as cruel and violent with the receiver as they like, as they can simply claim the receiver was being aggressive and needed subduing. Compare Attack Its Weak Point , with which it can overlap, and Shatterpoint Tap , which is for rigid objects rather than squishy human bodies. If the concept of pressure points pop up in video games, you can expect damage done to them to be a Critical Hit .
